raw_output_payload.reasoning: We need a bash command that searches for "hello" in all regular files in /workspace and display matched file name. Use grep -rl "hello" /workspace. That prints file names. Ensure only regular files: grep by default searches files. Use find to restrict to regular files: find /workspace -type f -exec grep -q "hello" {} \; -print. But simpler: grep -rl "hello" /workspace. That will search all files, but may include binary? Use -I to ignore binary. So: grep -rlI "hello" /workspace. Output file names. Provide one line.
